name: Inogen Inc
business: Inogen, Inc. is a medical technology business that primarily focuses on respiratory health, developing, manufacturing, and marketing portable oxygen concentrators, the Simeox airway clearance device, the Voxi 5 stationary oxygen concentrator, and Aurora CPAP masks. The company sells to home medical equipment providers, distributors, resellers, and directly to consumers in the United States and internationally.
namedSuppliers: Jiangsu Yuyue Medical Equipment & Supply Co., Ltd.

exposures.sentence: Inogen, Inc. is a medical technology business that primarily focuses on respiratory health.

We have also launched Inogen Connect, a wireless connectivity platform for the Inogen One and Rove POCs consisting of a front-end mobile application for use by patients and a back-end database portal for use by homecare providers.

catalysts.event: Strategic Collaboration Agreement with Jiangsu Yuyue Medical Equipment & Supply Co., Ltd.
catalysts.sentence: On January 25, 2025, we entered into a Strategic Collaboration Agreement, or the Collaboration Agreement, with Jiangsu Yuyue Medical Equipment & Supply Co., Ltd., or Yuwell.

catalysts.event: Planned more extensive launch of Aurora CPAP masks
catalysts.sentence: A more extensive launch of these products is planned in 2026 as we focus on market development.

tamClaims.claim: An estimated 200 million people in the world have COPD
tamClaims.sentence: According to a report published by the Forum of International Respiratory Societies in 2022, an estimated 200 million people in the world have COPD.

tamClaims.claim: Prevalence of COPD among adults 18 years or older was approximately 6.1%
tamClaims.sentence: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, or CDC, in the United States estimates that prevalence of COPD among adults 18 years or older was approximately 6.1% based on CDC data of age-adjusted prevalence of COPD from 2011 to 2021.

tamClaims.claim: Total annual economic cost from COPD in the United States was projected to be approximately $50 billion
tamClaims.sentence: In terms of economic impact, the total annual economic cost from COPD in the United States was projected to be approximately $50 billion with nearly one million COPD emergency department visits each year.
